text:
Once upon an age somewhere far past cottonwoods bloomed deep russett rust. There nestled among their wispy throats was MargRET Owl.

Margaret was no ordinary owl. Once she could strut city halls. But then came feathers full of feathers of grey. That night she dreamt of whispers thin-as-web silk.

Whispers urged Margaret.

One feather stirred—

Margaret fluttered.

A songbird swooped—

Margaret hoot-ed.

They sang!

How Margaret loved them!

One bright-eyed robin blinked like stars.

The robin sang brighter—

Margaret clucked.

The stars twinkled more.

Margaret clucked harder—

That mockingjay—

Something swooped—

Margaret flutter-ed!

That's when.

She hoot-ed.

With song.

They hoot-ed together.

Together flew!

That's how.

Margaret became leader.

A wise old owl.

And her flock—

Robin stars,

Mockingjay—

Oh, just thrives.

Margaret knew.

It was
